Growing stock modeling workflow

Florian Franz

2026-05-04

  • 01 — Terrestrial sample plots (01_vol_sample_plots.R)
  • 02 — Point cloud metrics (02_forest_metrics.R)
  • 03 — Random forest training (03_model_train.R)
  • 04 — Wall-to-wall prediction (04_prediction.R)
  • 05 — Small area estimation (05_small_area_estimation.R)

This document documents the wall-to-wall growing stock modeling in the Solling area: it briefly describes each script and shows selected maps and plots built from the same outputs the scripts write.

Step Script Main outputs (under data/processed_data/ or output/)
01 scripts/01_vol_sample_plots.R vol_stp.RDS, vol_stp.gpkg
02 scripts/02_forest_metrics.R plot_metrics_pc_solling_incl_forest_type.RDS, metrics_w2w_solling_incl_forest_type.tif
03 scripts/03_model_train.R global_rf_model.rds, RF_full_model_variable_importance.csv
04 scripts/04_prediction.R output/global_rf_prediction.tif, vol_stp_vs_pred_vol.gpkg
05 scripts/05_small_area_estimation.R output/small_area_estimation_revier.csv, output/w2w_pred_small_area_metrics.tif

01 — Terrestrial sample plots (01_vol_sample_plots.R)

Phase-2 BI (Betriebsinventur) points and trees are cleaned, tree volumes are computed, and volume per hectare is aggregated to each sample point. Tabular results are written to vol_stp.RDS. Script 02 expects sample-plot geometries as vol_stp.gpkg.

Distribution of terrestrial growing stock (m³ ha⁻¹) at sample points.

02 — Point cloud metrics (02_forest_metrics.R)

Normalized image-based point clouds are summarized to plot-level metrics and a wall-to-wall metric stack. Dominant species from Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) forest type tiles is added. The metrics feed the random forest in step 03.

Mean canopy height (m) from the wall-to-wall metric stack in the Solling area.

03 — Random forest training (03_model_train.R)

A global random forest predicts plot-level growing stock from point cloud-derived metrics (and dominant_species when included). The script runs automated predictor subsetting and fits the final model.

Predictor importance (%IncMSE) from the variable-selection run (top 15).

04 — Wall-to-wall prediction (04_prediction.R)

The fitted model is applied to metrics_w2w_solling_incl_forest_type.tif. The raster output/global_rf_prediction.tif holds predicted growing stock (m³ ha⁻¹). Mean predicted values are extracted within buffered plots and stored in vol_stp_vs_pred_vol.gpkg for comparison with terrestrial volumes.

Predicted growing stock (m³ ha⁻¹) from the global random forest model.
Observed vs. predicted growing stock. Each predicted value is the spatial mean of the wall-to-wall prediction raster inside the terrestrial sample-plot buffer (13m).

05 — Small area estimation (05_small_area_estimation.R)

For Reviere in the selected forestry offices, the script combines design-based (stratified) estimates, raster model-based estimates, and GREG model-assisted estimates. Compared with the direct stratified estimates, the model-assisted (GREG) revier-level mean usually has a smaller standard error, because auxiliary wall-to-wall information is used to stabilize the estimate (see table below).

First 5 Reviere: direct stratified mean, model-based raster mean, and GREG model-assisted mean (full results in CSV).
Revier Direct mean (m³ ha⁻¹) Direct standard error (m³ ha⁻¹) Model-based mean (m³ ha⁻¹) Model-assisted mean (m³ ha⁻¹) Model-assisted standard error (m³ ha⁻¹)
254_11 272.8302 12.855899 307.5679 287.6962 9.352854
254_12 292.6320 13.924224 316.5714 289.9422 9.410623
254_13 315.8809 8.958859 311.8517 310.5089 7.304178
254_14 318.1654 14.098643 282.0562 279.5411 11.073861
254_16 332.5273 11.156908 308.1688 325.2055 8.958920
